name: ServiceNow, Inc.

profile.description: ServiceNow is the leading enterprise workflow automation platform with 29,187 employees. Q1 2026 subscription revenue of $3.67B (up 22% YoY), Now Assist AI suite tracking toward $1.5B ACV, and total revenue of $3.8B. Recently acquired Armis for $7.75B to expand cybersecurity capabilities.

innovation.label: STRONG
innovation.analysis: Track Record (3/3): Subscription revenue of $3.67B grew 22% YoY with Now Assist AI tracking toward $1.5B ACV (up from $1B target) [1]. Customers spending $1M+ on Now Assist grew 130% YoY. $7.75B Armis acquisition expands into cybersecurity. AI Deployment (2/2): Now Assist AI generating $1.5B ACV with specific enterprise customer adoption metrics. Market Share (2/2): Dominant enterprise workflow platform with 22.5% growth in remaining performance obligations. Product Pipeline (1/2): Raised full-year guidance to $15.755B midpoint for subscription revenue. R&D Intensity (1/1): Significant AI and platform R&D investment.

inspiration.label: DEVELOPING
inspiration.analysis: Mission Floor (3/4): 'Make the world work better for everyone' is validated by platform adoption across 7,700+ enterprises. Community Impact (1/2): ServiceNow Foundation and community programs. Employee Retention (0/2): 29,187 employees, 17% stock decline despite beats. Insider Buying (1/2): Some executive purchases.

infrastructure.label: STRONG
infrastructure.analysis: Mechanically scored. Revenue of $3.8B up 22% with 32% non-GAAP operating margin [1]. Forward P/E of 23.75 above target but justified by growth. PEG of 1.24 is reasonable. ROE of 16.07% exceeds target. D/E of 20.73% is very conservative. Profit margin of 12.59% below target due to GAAP adjustments.

ai_resilience.label: MODERATE
ai_resilience.analysis: AI Risk Protection (5/9): Enterprise workflow automation faces AI disruption risk — AI agents could directly automate workflows that ServiceNow helps manage [1]. However, ServiceNow's deep enterprise integration and data creates switching costs. AI Opportunity (1/1): Now Assist at $1.5B ACV growing 130%+ demonstrates massive AI monetization [1].

infinite_game.summary: ServiceNow has a genuine mission validated by enterprise adoption and demonstrates courageous bets through the $7.75B Armis acquisition. CEO Bill McDermott's vision of a workflow automation platform shows long-term thinking.

investment_thesis: ServiceNow's Now Assist AI suite tracking $1.5B ACV with 130%+ customer spending growth, Q1 subscription revenue of $3.67B (up 22%), and raised full-year guidance demonstrate AI-powered enterprise growth acceleration [1]. ROE of 16.07%, D/E of 20.73%, and PEG of 1.24 offer quality at reasonable value.
thesis_continued: Forward P/E of 23.75 and trailing P/E of 71x leave limited margin of safety [1]. Stock declined 17% despite beating metrics, suggesting the market questions AI monetization durability. The $7.75B Armis acquisition creates integration risk. Enterprise spending slowdowns could impact long deal cycles.
key_catalysts: Now Assist ACV accelerating beyond $1.5B as enterprise AI workflow automation reaches critical mass
Armis cybersecurity integration creating unified security-and-workflow platform
CRPO growth of 22.5% sustaining through 2026 as new enterprise wins accelerate
key_risks: Forward P/E of 23.75 requires sustained 20%+ growth — any deceleration would compress the multiple
AI agent platforms potentially disintermediating ServiceNow's workflow management role
$7.75B Armis acquisition integration risk and potential dilutive impact
sources: ServiceNow Q1 2026 Earnings Release — SEC 8-K Filing, April 2026
Yahoo Finance — NOW Key Statistics, accessed June 2026
